parts.title: A. Even-multiplicity is forced by weak CTL (no (H-orb))

checks.name: there are 5 metric-multiplicity patterns of 4 interior facets (integer partitions of 4)
pattern (4,): every class m-regular on 5 vertices realizable = True  (all-even = True)
checks.detail: even <=> a perfect class-matching on the 5*m slots exists

checks.name: pattern (3, 1): every class m-regular on 5 vertices realizable = False  (all-even = False)
checks.detail: even <=> a perfect class-matching on the 5*m slots exists

checks.name: pattern (2, 2): every class m-regular on 5 vertices realizable = True  (all-even = True)
checks.detail: even <=> a perfect class-matching on the 5*m slots exists

checks.name: pattern (2, 1, 1): every class m-regular on 5 vertices realizable = False  (all-even = False)
checks.detail: even <=> a perfect class-matching on the 5*m slots exists

checks.name: pattern (1, 1, 1, 1): every class m-regular on 5 vertices realizable = False  (all-even = False)
checks.detail: even <=> a perfect class-matching on the 5*m slots exists

checks.name: patterns KILLED unconditionally by parity (odd part present): (1,1,1,1), (2,1,1), (3,1)
no 1-regular and no 3-regular graph on 5 vertices (odd*odd = odd degree sum) -> singleton/triple metric classes impossible
C_5 (2-regular) and K_5 (4-regular) on 5 vertices DO exist -> even classes are realizable

parts.title: B. The parity reduction reproduces every (H-orb) singleton case

checks.name: orbit (1, 1, 1, 1) [Sym(P)=1 (all 4 distinct-area boundary multisets)]: singleton orbit -> odd metric class -> killed by PARITY (no H-orb)
orbit (2, 1, 1) [|Sym(P)|=2 with two fixed facets]: singleton orbit -> odd metric class -> killed by PARITY (no H-orb)
orbit (3, 1) [needs |Sym(P)|>=3 -- excluded a priori]: excluded a priori by |Sym(P)|<=2
orbit (2, 2) [|Sym(P)|=2, both interior orbits size 2]: even orbit (2,2) -> survives parity, goes to (H-coc)/residual
orbit (4,) [needs |Sym(P)|>=4 -- excluded a priori]: excluded a priori by |Sym(P)|<=2

=> the four distinct-boundary-area multisets (Sym(P)=1, orbit (1,1,1,1)) are killed by PARITY whenever the 4 interior facets are metrically distinct -- the paper's (H-orb) singleton step, now UNCONDITIONAL on that genericity

parts.title: C. Residual ledger: where (H-orb) genuinely remains

checks.name: genuine (2,2) ORBIT (sigma twist=1): orientation system UNSAT -> closed by (H-coc)
accidental even pattern (no sigma, twist=0): orientation system SATISFIABLE -> soft tools do NOT close it

RESIDUAL of (H-orb) is sharper than the blanket hypothesis: only the accidental even patterns above remain, to be excluded by a finite real-algebraic argument on the constrained piece (shared tooling with (H-Qb))

residual: (4)-pattern: all 4 interior facets congruent (one metric class).
accidental (2,2) with Sym(P)=1: two congruent interior-facet pairs not realised by any symmetry of P.

conclusion.mechanism: weak-CTL parity: metric-class multiplicity must be even; odd patterns (incl. all singleton orbits) die without (H-orb)
open_residual: (4)-pattern: all 4 interior facets congruent (one metric class).
accidental (2,2) with Sym(P)=1: two congruent interior-facet pairs not realised by any symmetry of P.
conclusion.residual_route: real-algebraic / SMT exclusion of accidental interior-facet congruences (with (H-Qb))
